mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% fall below the 50th percentile of historical returns for that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and declining inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) signals strong real economic activity without inflationary pressure, creating a favorable environment for equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO*CPIAUCSL) fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust economic expansion that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1 growth periods show negative or flat performance relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% periods exceed the 75th percentile of historical returns for that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals strong labor market demand, boosting corporate revenue and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS > 100k periods fall below the 30th percentile of historical returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, creating positive momentum in the S&P 500 over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST*RSAFS) explains less than 5% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During high-interest-rate environments (FEDFUNDS > 4%), monetary policy tightening reduces corporate borrowing capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 4% periods exceed the 60th percentile of historical returns for that regime.
